scope: cross-family parser-level required tool argument fail-closed guard
blocker_reduced: agentic tool-loop safety for models that emit native tool envelopes with missing required arguments

source_patch.behavior: complete-response parsers now apply the shared required-schema helper before returning tool calls for parser-family paths that previously accepted missing or empty required fields

covered_parser_paths.gemma4_native_and_hermes: Gemma4 native <|tool_call>call:name{...}<tool_call|> and Hermes JSON fallback
covered_parser_paths.glm47_xml_and_json: GLM-4.7 XML arg and JSON-in-tool_call formats

verification.command: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_hunyuan_tool_parser.py tests/test_gemma4_tool_parser.py tests/test_glm47_tool_parser.py tests/test_gemma3_tool_parser.py tests/test_xml_function_tool_parser.py -q

classification: Parser families covered by this patch no longer return executable tool calls with missing required schema fields in the tested native dialect paths. Required tool requests can retry or fail closed instead of forwarding {} arguments to coding harnesses.
not_proven: live same-model direct/gateway/tunnel raw SSE for every parser family
all auto/no-tool/tool-result continuation modes for every model family
N2 JANG_1L, which remains off-limits in this lane
other_agent_next: Use this source fix when rebuilding the bundled Python/app runtime.
For live proof, run direct/gateway/tunnel raw SSE captures per family with reasoning enabled and required/auto/no-tool/tool-result continuation.
Do not add argument synthesis or parser repair to hide model failures; missing required fields should stay fail-closed.
